name: Volgograd Oblast (Russia)

colors.symbolism: the heroism of the defenders of Stalingrad and the blood shed in the city's defence
the silhouette of The Motherland Calls statue

description: A horizontal bicolour of red over blue, bearing near the hoist the white silhouette of The Motherland Calls statue and a gold 'Hero City Volgograd' inscription.
history: The flag of Volgograd Oblast was adopted on 24 December 2008. Volgograd, formerly Stalingrad, holds the Soviet title of Hero City for its defence against the Wehrmacht in the Battle of Stalingrad (1942-1943); the statue The Motherland Calls stands on the Mamayev Kurgan above the city.

features.symbolism: the red over blue bicolour combines the historic Stalingrad colours of valour with the blue of the Volga River.
